lines.english: You Yourself created the counterfeit and the genuine.
You Yourself appraise all people.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਰੇ ਪਰਖਿ ਖਜਾਨੈ ਪਾਇਹਿ ਖੋਟੇ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: khare parakh khajaanai paaeihi khotte bharam bhulaavaniaa |6|
lines.english: You appraise the true, and place them in Your Treasury; You consign the false to wander in delusion. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੇ ਭਾਣੇ ਵਿਚਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਵਸੈ ਤੂੰ ਭਾਣੈ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਆਵਣਿਆ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: tere bhaane vich amrit vasai toon bhaanai amrit peeaavaniaa |7|
lines.english: In Your Sweet Will, the Amrit is found; by Your Will, You inspire us to drink in this Amrit. ||7||
By Guru's Grace, I praise You through the Word of the Shabad.
How can I behold You? How can I praise You?
The Shabad is Amrit; the Lord's Bani is Amrit.
Serving the True Guru, it permeates the heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਪੀ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਸਭ ਭੁਖ ਲਹਿ ਜਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੮॥੧੫॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: naanak amrit naam sadaa sukhadaataa pee amrit sabh bhukh leh jaavaniaa |8|15|16|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Ambrosial Naam is forever the Giver of peace; drinking in this Amrit, all hunger is satisfied. ||8||15||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀ ਸਦਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਸੇ ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਬੁਝਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: amrit pee sadaa tripataase kar kirapaa trisanaa bujhaavaniaa |1|
lines.english: Those who drink it in are satisfied forever. Showering His Mercy upon them, the Lord quenches their thirst. ||1||
Maajh, Third Mehl:
How rare are those Gurmukhs who find it.
The Ambrosial Nectar rains down, softly and gently.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਵਾਰੀ ਜੀਉ ਵਾਰੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਆਵਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: hau vaaree jeeo vaaree guramukh amrit peeaavaniaa |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice, my soul is a sacrifice, to those Gurmukhs who drink in this Ambrosial Nectar.
By Guru's Grace, intuitive understanding is obtained;

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਸਨਾ ਰਸੁ ਚਾਖਿ ਸਦਾ ਰਹੈ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤੀ ਸਹਜੇ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: rasanaa ras chaakh sadaa rahai rang raatee sahaje har gun gaavaniaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The tongue tastes the essence, and remains forever imbued with the Lord's Love, intuitively singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਮਾਰੇ ਇਕਸੁ ਸਿਉ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: dubidhaa maare ikas siau liv laae |
lines.english: subduing the sense of duality, they are in love with the One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਤਾ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ਨਦਰੀ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: nadar kare taa har gun gaavai nadaree sach samaavaniaa |2|
lines.english: When He bestows His Glance of Grace, then they sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord; by His Grace, they merge in Truth. ||2||
Above all is Your Glance of Grace, O God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਝ ਤੇ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਕਿਛੁ ਨ ਹੋਵੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੋਝੀ ਪਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: tujh te baahar kichh na hovai guramukh sojhee paavaniaa |3|
lines.english: Without You, nothing happens at all; the Gurmukhs understand this. ||3||
Upon some it is bestowed less, and upon others it is bestowed more.
Your Treasures are overflowing with Ambrosial Nectar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸੇਵੇ ਕੋਈ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਪਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: bin satigur seve koee na paavai gur kirapaa te paavaniaa |4|
lines.english: Without serving the True Guru, no one obtains it. It is obtained only by Guru's Grace. ||4||
The Gurmukhs contemplate the essence of reality;
Those who serve the True Guru are beautiful.
The Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Lord, entices their inner minds.
The deluded, self-willed manmukhs are ruined through the love of duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਬਾਣੀ ਰਤਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਣਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: amrit man tan baanee rataa amrit sehaj sunaavaniaa |5|
lines.english: Their minds and bodies are attuned to the Ambrosial Bani of the Word; this Ambrosial Nectar is intuitively heard. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਸਦਾ ਵਿਸਟਾ ਮਹਿ ਵਾਸਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੇਵਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਗਵਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: anadin sadaa visattaa meh vaasaa bin sevaa janam gavaavaniaa |6|
lines.english: Night and day, they continually sit in manure. Without selfless service, their lives are wasted away. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਲੇਵੈ ਮਰੈ ਬਿਖੁ ਖਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: naam na levai marai bikh khaae |
lines.english: They do not chant the Naam, and they die, eating poison.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਵੈ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਆਪਿ ਪੀਆਏ ॥
lines.translit: amrit peevai jis no aap peeae |
lines.english: They alone drink in this Amrit, whom the Lord Himself inspires to do so.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਨ ਪੂਰਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸਭ ਆਪੇ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਨਦਰੀ ਆਵਣਿਆ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: pooran poor rahiaa sabh aape guramat nadaree aavaniaa |7|
lines.english: The Perfect Lord is Himself perfectly pervading everywhere; through the Guru's Teachings, He is perceived. ||7||
By Guru's Grace, they intuitively enshrine love for the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਮਾਲਿ ਸਦਾ ਤੂੰ ਸਹਜੇ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੮॥੧੬॥੧੭॥
lines.translit: naanak naam samaal sadaa toon sahaje sach samaavaniaa |8|16|17|
lines.english: O Nanak, remember the Naam forever, and you shall merge into the True One with intuitive ease. ||8||16||17||
He who has created, shall Himself destroy.
He Himself is the Immaculate Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇ ਸਚਿ ਲਾਗੇ ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: se sach laage jo tudh bhaae |
lines.english: Those who please You are linked to the Truth.
Maajh, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਸਚਾ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਸਚੈ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: sachai sabad sachaa saalaahee sachai mel milaavaniaa |1|
lines.english: Through the True Word of the Shabad, they praise the True One, and they merge in the merging of Truth. ||1||
They serve the True One forever, with intuitive ease.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਵਾਰੀ ਜੀਉ ਵਾਰੀ ਸਚੁ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: hau vaaree jeeo vaaree sach saalaahaniaa |
lines.english: I am a sacrifice, my soul is a sacrifice, to those who praise the True One.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਧਿਆਇਨਿ ਸੇ ਸਚਿ ਰਾਤੇ ਸਚੇ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਵਣਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sach dhiaaein se sach raate sache sach samaavaniaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Those who meditate on the True One are attuned to Truth; they are absorbed into the Truest of the True. ||1||Pause||
The True One is everywhere, wherever I look.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨੁ ਸਚਾ ਰਸਨਾ ਸਚਿ ਰਾਤੀ ਸਚੁ ਸੁਣਿ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਨਣਿਆ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: tan sachaa rasanaa sach raatee sach sun aakh vakhaananiaa |2|
lines.english: True are the bodies of those whose tongues are attuned to Truth. They hear the Truth, and speak it with their mouths. ||2||
By Guru's Grace, I enshrine Him in my mind.
